name: Hypotension
info: ● Defined as SBP < 70 + 2 x (age in years)

info: ● 200 mcg / 2 mL requires dilution before administration

name: Epinephrine Nebulized
info: ● 2.5-5 mg Epi (1 mg/mL,1:1,000) mixed with 4-5 mL Normal Saline OR
● Weight-Based: 0.5 mg/kg Epi (1 mg/mL, 1:1000) (Only for use in young infants, max 5 mg)

name: Epinephrine Nebulized
info: ● 2.5-5 mg Epi (1 mg/mL,1:1,000) mixed with 4-5 mL Normal Saline OR
● Weight-Based: 0.5 mg/kg Epi (1 mg/mL, 1:1000) (Only for use in young infants, max 5 mg)

name: Epinephrine Nebulized

name: Metoclopromide PO
info: ● comes as 1 mg/mL solution, or 5 and 10 mg tabs, max dose 10 mg

name: Ondansetron ODT PO
info: oral dissolving tablet, comes as 4 mg and 8 mg tabs, max dose 8 mg

name: Promethazine  IM/IV
info: ●  IV/IM is generally avoided due to high risk of tissue injury.
●  If used, promethazine should be administered diluted and infused over 10-15 minutes or at a rate not to exceed 25 mg/min.
● Adminster through a large bore vein (not hand or wrist) or via a running IV line at port farthest from patient's vein to reduce risk of phlebitis.

name: Promethazine PO/PR
info: This is also the rectal dose. PO also available in 12.5,25, and 50 mg tabs. Max dose 25 mg

name: Dexamethasone
info: ● Dosing for Peri-Operative nausea and vomiting

name: Acyclovir  IV/IO
info: ● For most common infections including Varicella, herpes zoster, HSV encephalitis
● higher doses may be indicated in certain conditions.

name: Ampicillin IV/IO/IM
info: ● Dosing is complex for neonates and depends on gestational and postnatal age.
● Max dose can upwards of 8-12 grams/day.

name: Gentamicin IV/IO/IM
info: ● Option 1: (Initial dose) adhere to policy guidelines, specific indications, and level to guide further dosing. For CNS infection, intra-abdominal infection, UTI, endocarditis
● Option 2: Represents extended-interval dosing. Please refer to your institutional guidelines.

name: Gentamicin IV/IO/IM
info: ● Option 1: (Initial dose) adhere to policy guidelines, specific indications, and level to guide further dosing. For CNS infection, intra-abdominal infection, UTI, endocarditis

info: ● Option 1: (Initial dose) adhere to policy guidelines, specific indications, and level to guide further dosing. For CNS infection, intra-abdominal infection, UTI, endocarditis

name: Metronidazole IV
info: 30 mg/kg/day IV Q6 hr (7.5 mg/kg/dose), max dose 4 grams/day

name: Vancomycin IV/IO
info: ● Initial dosing presented, adhere to policy and institutional guidelines, specific indications, and levels to guide further dosing.

name: Doxycycline PO
info: Doxycycline binds to calcium less readily than other tetracyclines, has not been shown to cause teeth staining

name: Zanamvir Inhaled
info: not recommended < 7 years old  Prophylaxis duration 7 days, not recommended < 5 years old

name: Zanamivir Inhaled (Prophylaxis))
info: Influenza prophylaxis, duration 7 days, not recommended < 5 years old

name: Epinephrine 1 mg/mL (1:1000) Endotracheal
info: ● Follow with saline flush or dilute in 1-5 mL isotonic saline

name: Adenosine IV/IO
info: ● Subsequent doses 0.2 mg/kg (Max 12 mg), give RAPIDLY
● Follow immediately with 5-10 mL saline flush

name: Adenosine IV/IO
info: ● Subsequent doses 0.2 mg/kg (Max 12 mg), give RAPIDLY
● Follow immediately with 5-10 mL saline flush

name: Amiodarone IV/IO
info: ● For perfusing rhythm: give over 20-60 minutes.
● For V-Fib or pulseless V-Tach, give as rapid bolus.

name: Atropine IV/IO
info: ● May repeat dose once in 5 minutes.
● Max total dose for child in 1 mg, max total dose for adolescent is 3 mg.

name: Atropine Endotracheal
info: ● Max total dose for child is 1 mg, max total dose for adolescent is 3 mg.

name: Calcium Gluconate 10% IV/IO
info: ● Substitute for Calcium Chloride if non-emergent situation or no central access.
● In emergencies (cardiac arrest), Calcium Chloride is recommended.

name: Cardioversion
info: ● Subsequent settings if first dose ineffective

name: Cardioversion
info: ● Subsequent settings if first dose ineffective

name: Lidocaine Infusion IV/IO
info: 4 mg/mL in D5W or 8 mg/mL in D5W

name: Magnesium Sulfate IV/IO
info: ● Give over 10-20 minutes in torsades or V-tach with pulse, asthma, hypomagnesemia
● Give as bolus over 1-2 minutes in pulseless rhythm.

name: Procainamide IV/IO
info: ● Give over 30-60 minutes as slow IV injection in solution, monitor QT interval.

name: Phenylephrine Bolus IV/IO
info: ● Mix 1 mL (10 mg) of Phenylephrine (10 mg/mL) into 100 mL NS

name: Ibuprofen PO
info: ● Max single dose 400 mg, max daily dose 2400 mg

name: Maintenance Infusion Rate
info: 4 mL/kg for 1st 10 kg of weight, + 2 mL/kg for next 10 kg weight, plus 1 mL/kg after that.

name: D10 IV/IO
info: ● D10 is preferred for all pediatric patients

name: D10 IV/IO
info: ● D10 is preferred for all pediatric patients

info: ● Infants < 12 months: massage into buccal mucosa, repeat if still hypoglycemic after 30 minutes
● Children > 12 months: repeat dose in 10-15 minutes if still hypoglycemic

name: "Ketofol" IV
